expectations.evidence: No sub-skill file is loaded or referenced. The approach works without any skill framework.

expectations.text: Extracts at least 4 action items from the notes
expectations.evidence: Extracted exactly 4 action items: (1) DB query optimization for Sarah, (2) monitoring dashboards for Mike, (3) integration tests for Lisa, (4) fix checkout bug for Mike.

expectations.text: Identifies Sarah, Mike, and Lisa as assignees
expectations.evidence: Table lists Sarah Chen, Mike Rodriguez, and Lisa Park as assignees across the 4 items.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs lookup-user for each unique assignee
expectations.evidence: Does not mention jira.mjs. Describes needing REST API calls to resolve names to account IDs but cannot execute them.

expectations.text: Presents a table or list of extracted tasks for user confirmation before creating
expectations.evidence: Presents a table of 4 action items with #, Action Item, Assignee, Priority, and Due Date columns, plus detailed ticket breakdown.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs create for each confirmed task with --parent or appropriate flags
expectations.evidence: Does not mention jira.mjs. Shows a REST API JSON payload example but no jira.mjs create commands.
No reference to action-item-patterns.md or ticket-writing-guide.md anywhere in the approach.
